prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "高抬腿" (high knees) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person performing a high-knee drill at peak knee lift: right leg supporting with foot in mid-stride contact with the ground; left knee driven up explosively to hip height or higher with thigh at or above parallel to the floor and shin perpendicular; arms in running-style opposition (right arm forward bent at 90 degrees, left arm back bent at 90 degrees); torso upright with slight forward lean; plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: hip flexors (psoas major and rectus femoris) of the lifted leg, and calf muscles of the supporting leg. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: gluteus maximus of the supporting leg and core muscles. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "髂腰肌 / 股直肌" pointing to the hip flexors of the lifted leg; "小腿三头肌" pointing to the calf muscles of the supporting leg; "臀大肌 / 核心" pointing to the glute and core area. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
